seoTitle: How to Use Jira Timeline for Project Scheduling
metaDescription: Learn how to navigate the Jira Timeline view, adjust schedules, track blockers, and filter tasks so your team stays on top of project deadlines.
intro: When daily tasks feel disconnected from bigger deadlines, it's easy to lose sight of how work actually fits together. This guide explains how to use the Jira Timeline view to organize epics, adjust schedules, spot blockers, and filter the board so it stays easy to scan.

steps.heading: Open the timeline hub
steps.body: Navigate to the left sidebar menu in your Jira software space and select Timeline to bring up the scheduling view.

steps.heading: Choose your view scale
steps.body: Use the settings option to switch the timeline between weeks, months, or quarters depending on how far ahead you're planning.

steps.heading: Expand epics to see nested tasks
steps.body: Click the dropdown next to any epic to reveal the standard tasks and child work items sitting underneath it.

steps.heading: Adjust dates and track blockers
steps.body: Drag the edges of a schedule bar to update due dates, and connect related issues to track dependencies that could delay delivery.

steps.heading: Filter by assignee or status
steps.body: Use the top filters bar to isolate specific team members or issue statuses so you can quickly check real-time progress.

faq.question: How do I access the Timeline view in Jira?
faq.answer: Open your Jira Cloud space and select the Timeline option from the left sidebar menu.

faq.question: Can I change the Jira Timeline to show months or quarters instead of weeks?
faq.answer: Yes, the settings option in the timeline lets you switch the view scale between weeks, months, or quarters.

faq.question: How do I update a task's due date on the Timeline?
faq.answer: Click and drag the edges of the task's schedule bar directly on the calendar grid to adjust its dates.

faq.question: Can I see which tasks are blocking others in the Timeline view?
faq.answer: Yes, you can connect related issues to track dependencies and blockers directly within the timeline.
